steps.title: Match the triangle angles
steps.content: Since $AB=AC$, triangle $ABC$ is isosceles, so its base angles are congruent. Because $E$ is on $AB$ and $F$ is on $AC$, the angle at $B$ in triangle $BED$ equals the angle at $C$ in triangle $CFD$. The problem also gives a second pair of equal angles.

steps.title: Use similar triangles
steps.content: With two pairs of congruent angles, triangles $BED$ and $CFD$ are similar. The corresponding side ratio is $$\frac{BE}{CF}=\frac{BD}{CD}$$

steps.title: Apply the side ratio
steps.content: Since $BD=\frac{5}{9}BC$, the remaining segment is $CD=\frac{4}{9}BC$. Therefore, $$\frac{10}{CF}=\frac{5}{4}$$ Solving gives $5CF=40$, so $CF=8$. The answer is A.
